2603.10828.title: BALD-SAM: Disagreement-based Active Prompting in Interactive Segmentation
2603.10828.paper_summary: The Segment Anything Model (SAM) has revolutionized interactive segmentation through spatial prompting. While existing work primarily focuses on automating prompts in various settings, real-world annotation workflows involve iterative refinement where annotators observe model outputs and strategically place prompts to resolve ambiguities. Current pipelines typically rely on the annotator's visual assessment of the predicted mask quality. We postulate that a principled approach for automated interactive prompting is to use a model-derived criterion to identify the most informative region for the next prompt. In this work, we establish active prompting: a spatial active learning approach where locations within images constitute an unlabeled pool and prompts serve as queries to prioritize information-rich regions, increasing the utility of each interaction. We further present BALD-SAM: a principled framework adapting Bayesian Active Learning by Disagreement (BALD) to spatial prompt selection by quantifying epistemic uncertainty. To do so, we freeze the entire model and apply Bayesian uncertainty modeling only to a small learned prediction head, making intractable uncertainty estimation practical for large multi-million parameter foundation models. Across 16 datasets spanning natural, medical, underwater, and seismic domains, BALD-SAM demonstrates strong cross-domain performance, ranking first or second on 14 of 16 benchmarks. We validate these gains through a comprehensive ablation suite covering 3 SAM backbones and 35 Laplace posterior configurations, amounting to 38 distinct ablation settings. Beyond strong average performance, BALD-SAM surpasses human prompting and, in several categories, even oracle prompting, while consistently outperforming one-shot baselines in final segmentation quality, particularly on thin and structurally complex objects.
2603.10828.paper_summary_zh: 段落任何模型（SAM）透過空間提示徹底改變了互動分割。雖然現有的工作主要集中在各種環境中自動化提示，但現實世界的標註工作流程涉及迭代精煉，標註者觀察模型輸出並策略性地放置提示以解決模糊性。當前的管道通常依賴於標註者對預測掩碼質量的視覺評估。我們假設，自動化互動提示的原則性方法是使用模型衍生的標準來識別下一個提示的最具信息量的區域。在這項工作中，我們建立了主動提示：一種空間主動學習方法，其中圖像中的位置構成一個未標記的池，提示作為查詢以優先考慮信息豐富的區域，增加每次互動的效用。我們進一步提出了BALD-SAM：一個原則性框架，通過量化認知不確定性將貝葉斯主動學習（BALD）適應於空間提示選擇。為此，我們凍結整個模型，僅對一個小的學習預測頭應用貝葉斯不確定性建模，使得對大型數百萬參數基礎模型的不可處理不確定性估計變得實用。跨越涵蓋自然、醫療、水下和地震領域的16個數據集，BALD-SAM展示了強大的跨領域性能，在16個基準中有14個排名第一或第二。我們通過一個全面的消融套件驗證這些增益，涵蓋3個SAM骨幹和35個拉普拉斯後驗配置，共計38個不同的消融設置。除了強大的平均性能外，BALD-SAM超越了人類提示，在幾個類別中甚至超越了神諭提示，同時在最終分割質量上持續超越一次性基準，特別是在細長和結構複雜的物體上。
2603.10828.author: Prithwijit Chowdhury et.al.
2603.10828.authors: Prithwijit Chowdhury, Mohit Prabhushankar, Ghassan AlRegib
